sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 2.45 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.2)

statement.latex: b) (P.\,Hall). Prove or refute the following conjecture: If the marginal subgroup$v^*G$has finite index$m$in$G$, then the order of$vG$is finite and divides a power of$m$. Editors' comment:\/ There are examples when$|vG|$does not divide a power of$m$(Yu.\,G.\,Kleiman, Trans. Moscow Math. Soc., { 1983}, no.\,2, 63--110), but the question of finiteness remains open. { Yu.\,I.\,Merzlyakov}

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
